author: ryanravn
description: Sync notes across devices using Seafile. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: ryanravn
manifest.description: Sync notes across devices using Seafile.
manifest.name: Seafile Continued

name: Seafile Continued

releases.author: ryanravn
releases.description:
## Fix: encrypted libraries are no longer silently un-encrypted on first sync

In 0.3.9 and earlier, the first sync against an encrypted Seafile library would corrupt it: the plugin uploaded commits without the encryption metadata fields, and the server uses the head commit to determine encryption status, so the library flipped to \`encrypted: false\` on the server. Any blocks already encrypted on disk became unreadable through the API.

0.3.10 includes:
- \`encrypted: "true"\`, \`enc_version\`, \`magic\`, \`key\`, and (when applicable) \`salt\` in uploaded commits when syncing to an encrypted library.
- Uses the correct field name \`key\` for the wrapped block key (Seafile's wire format uses \`key\` in commit JSON, even though the same value is exposed as \`random_key\` on \`/download-info/\`).

Verified live against a Seafile server: a fresh encrypted library remains \`encrypted: true\` after a patched sync, where 0.3.9 would have flipped it to \`encrypted: false\`.

**Caveat for existing users:** if any of your previously-encrypted libraries were already corrupted by 0.3.9 (or earlier), they cannot be recovered. The head commit on the server lost the encryption metadata. Create a fresh encrypted library and move files into it. Verify with \`/api/v2.1/repos/\` — if it reports \`encrypted: false\` for a library you thought was encrypted, that's the symptom.

releases.author: ryanravn
releases.description:
## Maintenance
Addresses findings from the Obsidian community plugin portal review:

- Drop deprecated build deps (`builtin-modules`, `fs-extra`, `node-fetch`, `eslint-plugin-import`). Replaced with Node built-ins.
- Drop the stale `pnpm-lock.yaml` and regenerate `package-lock.json` with npm.
- Enable issues on the repository so users can report bugs and request features.

releases.author: ryanravn
releases.description:
## Fixes
- **Fix ENOENT crash on sync after id rename.** `PLUGIN_DIR` was hardcoded to `plugins/seafile`, so the plugin tried to read and write its sync data at a path that didn't exist after the rename to `seafile-continued`. Now derived from `manifest.id`.
- **Fix ENOENT on first sync.** Obsidian's `adapter.append()` throws if the file doesn't exist; the sync data log was only created later. Now `SyncController.init()` creates empty `sync_dlog` and `sync_data` files up front.

releases.author: ryanravn
releases.description:
First release of the continued fork.

## Highlights
- **Encrypted repository support** (enc_version 2 and 4): passphrase prompt on repo selection and on Obsidian restart, never stored in plaintext.
- **Manual sync**: "Sync now" button in settings and a "Seafile: Sync now" command (assignable to a hotkey).

## Credits
Original plugin by [@conql](https://github.com/conql). Continuation maintained by [@ryanravn](https://github.com/ryanravn).
